title: The Pittsburgh Asian Kitchens Creators Keep Returning To
subtitle: Sushi, noodles, Thai, Indian, Japanese.
angle: the Asian kitchens generating the most creator coverage this quarter. The dishes the city is filming and the rooms behind them.
intro:
Everyday Noodles in Squirrel Hill has no Instagram, no TikTok, and creators have already filmed inside. Two of them this quarter. Three thousand plays. That's what happens when the room is good enough that a missing feed doesn't matter.

Pittsburgh's Asian kitchens are getting steady creator attention this spring. The kitchens haven't all caught up. Pusadee's Garden holds first among Pittsburgh Thai on conversation volume. Its feed posts twice a month and nothing on short-form video. Mola in East Liberty leads sushi on review depth alone, on a feed that has gone nearly quiet. Atarashi in Oakland holds the same top sushi rank, but the recent reviews are pulling sentiment in a complicated direction.

The pattern is clear. Pittsburgh is pointing cameras at its Asian kitchens faster than the kitchens have built the surface to catch them. Issue 02 will track who closed the gap.

items.name: Pusadee's Garden
items.descriptor: First in Pittsburgh Thai. Feed posts twice a month, no TikTok account.

featured_tiktok.author: mad_i_libs
featured_tiktok.caption:
This Thai restaurant is a gorgeous spot for your next date night or special occasion in Pittsburgh! 💖 

There are so many food and drink options, for meat-eaters and vegans alike, you'll want to come back more than once. 🥂 

Tip: Show up

items.name: Everyday Noodles
items.descriptor: Second in restaurants on review depth. No Instagram, no TikTok, creators filming anyway.

featured_tiktok.author: sheseatingit
featured_tiktok.caption:
Everyday Noodles is my dream come true because I could eat noodles everyday.

#Pittsburgh #soupdumplings #xiaolongbao #pennsylvania #pittsburghsteelers

items.name: Mola
items.descriptor: First in Pittsburgh sushi on review depth. The feed has gone nearly quiet.

featured_tiktok.caption:
You already know .. do I even need to say anymore about how much I love Mola🍣🥢 my favorite sushi in Pittsburgh

items.name: Atarashi
items.descriptor: First in Pittsburgh sushi. The recent reviews are pulling the other way.

items.name: Bengal Kabab House & Restaurant
items.descriptor: First in Pittsburgh Indian, 74th in restaurants. The buffet has no feed.

items.name: Benihana - Pittsburgh
items.descriptor: 37th in restaurants on review depth. No social to catch what happens at every table.

items.name: Bombay To Burgh
items.descriptor: First in Pittsburgh Indian, 26th in restaurants. The social layer hasn't arrived.

items.name: Chao Phraya Thai Cuisine
items.descriptor: First in its tier. Zero photos and no hours on Google hold the ceiling down.
